zh.lifeSummary: 當Gilbert Ier de Gévaudan於1055年出生在法國藍多-盧希隆加爾米約時，他的父親Berenger de Millau35歲，母親Adèle de Carlat Viscountess of Carlat34歲。他與Gerberge comtesse de Provence結婚。他們至少是4個女兒的父母。他去世於1107年在法國加爾米約享年52歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Gévaudan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French (Gévaudan): habitational name from Gévaudan, a region in Lozère, named after the Gabali, a Gallic tribe. Compare Gevedon and Jividen , and also Givaudan .

History: The surname Gévaudan was brought to North America by various immigrants from France. But descendants of the first immigrant with this name, a Huguenot from southern France, bear altered forms of the name (see Jividen ).

variants.name: Jividen
meanings: Altered form of French Gévaudan (see Gevaudan ). Compare Gividen .

History: The Jividens trace their origin to Antoine Gévaudan alias Jividen, a Huguenot from southern France, who in 1726 died in Manakin, VA. In the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors he is listed with the original form of his surname, Gévaudan (along with its variant Givaudan ), while in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of the Founders of Manakin the common altered form Jividen is also found. His descendants also bear the surnames Geveden, Gevedon, and Gividen.

variants.name: Gevedon
meanings: Altered form of French Gévaudan (see Gevaudan ). See also Jividen .

nameMeanings.name: Gilbert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from gisil ‘pledge’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. This was the name of the founder of the only native British religious order (abolished at the Dissolution of the Monasteries), St Gilbert of Sempringham ( ?1083–1189 ), in whose honour it is still sometimes bestowed, especially among Roman Catholics. It gained a wider currency in the 19th century.

nameMeanings.name: Ier
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: and Norwegian vernacular form of   Peter .

variants.name: Peter
meanings: form of the name of the best-known of all Christ's apostles, traditionally regarded as the founder of the Christian Church. The name derives, via Latin, from Greek petros ‘stone, rock’. This is used as a translation of the Aramaic byname Cephas, given to the apostle Simon son of Jona, to distinguish him from another of the same name ( Simon Zelotes ). ‘When Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone’ (John 1:42). According to Matthew 16:17–18, Christ says more explicitly, ‘Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona … thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church’. The name has been in continuous use since the Middle Ages.

en.lifeSummary: When Gilbert Ier de Gévaudan was born in 1055, in Milhaud, Gard, Languedoc-Roussillon, France, his father, Berenger de Millau, was 35 and his mother, Adèle de Carlat Viscountess of Carlat, was 34. He married Gerberge comtesse de Provence. They were the parents of at least 4 daughters. He died in 1107, in Milhaud, Gard, France, at the age of 52.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Gilbert Ier de Gévaudan est né en 1055, à Milhaud, Gard, Languedoc-Roussillon, France, son père, Berenger de Millau, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Adèle de Carlat Viscountess of Carlat, avait 34 ans. Il a épousé Gerberge comtesse de Provence. Ils ont eu au moins 4 filles. Il est décédé en 1107, à Milhaud, Gard, France, à l’âge de 52 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Gilbert Ier de Gévaudan 1055 in Milhaud, Gard, Languedoc-Roussillon, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Berenger de Millau 35 und seine Mutter Adèle de Carlat Viscountess of Carlat 34. Er hat Gerberge comtesse de Provence geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Töchter. Er starb 1107 im Alter von 52 Jahren in Milhaud, Gard, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Gilbert Ier de Gévaudan è nato il nel 1055 nel Milhaud, Gard, Languedoc-Roussillon, Francia, il suo padre, Berenger de Millau, aveva 35 anni e il suo madre, Adèle de Carlat Viscountess of Carlat, aveva 34 anni. Lui sposato Gerberge comtesse de Provence. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figlie. Morì nel 1107, nel Milhaud, Gard, Francia, all'età di 52 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Gilbert Ier de Gévaudanが1055年にフランスラングエドク-ロウシロンガードミルハウドで生まれた時，父Berenger de Millauは35歳，母Adèle de Carlat Viscountess of Carlatは34歳であった。Gerberge comtesse de Provenceと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の娘の親であった。彼は1107年にフランスガードミルハウドで52歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Gilbert Ier de Gévaudan(이)가 1055년 프랑스 랭도c-루시런 가드 미하드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Berenger de Millau(은)는 35세였고, 그의 어머니 Adèle de Carlat Viscountess of Carlat(은)는 34세였다. 그는 Gerberge comtesse de Provence(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1107년 프랑스 가드 미하드에서 52세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Gilbert Ier de Gévaudan nasceu em 1055, em Milhaud, Gard, Languedoc-Roussillon, França, seu pai, Berenger de Millau, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Adèle de Carlat Viscountess of Carlat, tinha 34 anos. Ele casou-se com Gerberge comtesse de Provence. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1107, em Milhaud, Gard, França, com 52 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Gilbert Ier de Gévaudan родился в 1055 г. в местности милхауд, гард, лангуедок-руссиллон, Франция, его отцу по имени Berenger de Millau было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Adèle de Carlat Viscountess of Carlat, 34. Он женился на женщине по имени Gerberge comtesse de Provence. У них было по крайней мере 4 дочери. Он умер в 1107 г. в местности милхауд, гард, Франция в возрасте 52 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Gilbert Ier de Gévaudan nació en 1055, en Milhaud, Gard, Languedoc-Roussillon, Francia, su papá, Berenger de Millau, tenía 35 años y su mamá, Adèle de Carlat Viscountess of Carlat, tenía 34 años. Se casó con Gerberge comtesse de Provence. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijas. Murió en 1107, en Milhaud, Gard, Francia, a la edad de 52 años.
